title: My best days begin with calm
subtitle: A present-tense affirmation for choosing how the morning starts.
excerpt: A short affirmation from Day 21 of the SLEEP program. For when the rush is a default rather than a necessity, and you want to replace it with something that works better.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 21 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

content.text: A short affirmation from Day 21 of the SLEEP program. The line works as permission, a reminder that a slow start is a productive one, and that calm is not the enemy of output.
I begin the day on my own terms. Calm mornings make calm choices. I choose peace as my first act of the day.
Say it three times. The third pass, say it after you've taken one slow breath. That breath is the first act.
Adapted from Day 21 of SLEEP: A 30-Day Tapping Intensive (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
